page: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Dlsv7 family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server type does not include any bundled local storage, but it is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external high-performance block storage. There are no integrated GPUs on this instance, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or direct GPU-based machine learning workloads. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D96ls_v7 is positioned as a compute-heavy, low-memory instance. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require massive parallel processing power without the overhead of large memory footprints. It is suited for compute-intensive tasks such as batch processing, high-performance computing simulations, large-scale compilation, and web serving workloads that do not demand high memory-per-core ratios.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance from the Dlsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low-memory configuration of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable for attaching external block storage. Designed for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, this server type is suited for parallel processing, batch computing, and large-scale web serving. Its resource allocation provides a cost-efficient option for raw CPU capacity relative to memory density, allowing users to scale compute power without paying for unused memory.

tagline: A high-core compute-optimized instance featuring ninety-six dedicated processors and a lean memory profile for intensive parallel workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
